variants: Quetzali Paredes is recognized for her accomplishment in obtaining a Lightning Pansori License, reflecting her skill in a traditional form of performance art. Her academic pursuits were guided by the influence of her doctoral advisor, Aarav Tomashevsky. Throughout her career, she has published her research in the Barley Moon Review, contributing valuable insights to her field. In addition to her academic endeavors, she actively volunteers as an Induction Advisor Plainspearl Neighbors, helping individuals in her community. The advancements in her studies have been supported by the Ivorygate Scholars Consortium, which provided essential funding for her research.
